question_text: A student rubs a balloon with a cloth and it becomes negatively charged. The balloon is then held near a wall and sticks to it. Explain why the balloon attracts to the wall, even though the wall is neutral (uncharged).
correct_answer: The negative charge on the balloon repels electrons in the wall, leaving the surface of the wall positively charged. Opposite charges attract, so the balloon sticks to the wall.
explanation: When a negatively charged object gets close to a neutral conductor like a wall, it pushes away (repels) the electrons in the wall. This leaves the near surface positively charged. Since opposite charges attract, the balloon is pulled towards the wall.
